name: Ziggurat Ur
description: The Ziggurat of Ur, also known as the Great Ziggurat of Ur, was a massive stepped pyramid located in the ancient city of Ur (modern-day Iraq). Dedicated to the moon god Nanna (Sin), it served as a temple and a focal point for religious and civic life. Its construction, initiated by Ur-Nammu and completed by his son Shulgi during the Third Dynasty of Ur (c. 21st century BCE), represented a significant feat of engineering and architectural prowess. The ziggurat symbolized the connection between the earthly and divine realms, with the temple at its summit believed to be the dwelling place of the god. Priests and priestesses performed rituals and offerings to appease Nanna and ensure the city's prosperity. The structure's imposing height and tiered design were intended to inspire awe and reverence, reinforcing the power of the ruling dynasty and the importance of religious devotion. It played a vital role in Sumerian cosmology, representing a cosmic mountain linking heaven and earth. The ziggurat's location in the heart of Ur made it a central hub for trade, administration, and social gatherings, further solidifying its significance in Sumerian society. Over time, the Ziggurat of Ur became a powerful symbol of Sumerian civilization, its legacy enduring through its architectural grandeur and its prominent place in Mesopotamian history and religious beliefs. Its ruin still stands today, a testament to the ingenuity and spiritual devotion of the Sumerian people.
subtitle: Temple to Nanna, Link to Gods

keyMyths.title: Dedication to Nanna
keyMyths.description: The Ziggurat of Ur was dedicated to Nanna, the moon god, believed to reside in the temple at its peak. Rituals and offerings were performed to appease the god and ensure the city's prosperity.

keyMyths.title: Construction by Ur-Nammu
keyMyths.description: The ziggurat's construction was initiated by King Ur-Nammu, who sought to create a monumental structure that would symbolize his power and piety. His son Shulgi completed the project, solidifying its importance.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: The Sumerians: Their History, Culture, and Character
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization, including religious practices and architecture.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization, including religious practices and architecture.

sources.author: Gwendolyn Leick
